steps.work: Two figures are congruent if they have exactly the same size and the same shape.
steps.explanation: Congruent means identical — one could be placed exactly on top of the other with no gaps.

steps.instruction: Triangle A has sides 3, 4, 5 cm. Triangle B has sides 3, 4, 5 cm. Are they congruent?
steps.work: All three sides match: 3 = 3, 4 = 4, 5 = 5. Yes, they are congruent.
steps.explanation: When all corresponding sides (and angles) match, the figures are congruent.

steps.instruction: Can congruent figures look different in position?
steps.work: Yes. Sliding, rotating, or flipping a shape does not change congruence. Only stretching or shrinking does.
steps.explanation: Congruent figures may face different directions or be in different locations, but they remain the same size and shape.

title: Same Area Does Not Mean Congruent

steps.instruction: Rectangle A is 2 × 8 cm. Rectangle B is 4 × 4 cm. Compare them.
steps.work: A: area = 16 sq cm, perimeter = 20 cm. B: area = 16 sq cm, perimeter = 16 cm.
steps.explanation: Both have the same area (16 sq cm).

steps.work: No. A is 2 × 8 and B is 4 × 4. Different dimensions, different shape.
steps.explanation: Same area does not mean same shape. These rectangles are not congruent.

steps.work: ALL corresponding sides AND angles must match. Area or perimeter alone is not enough.
steps.explanation: Congruence requires exact match of all measurements, not just one property like area.
